files.markdownDescription:
Input files or glob patterns containing JSON-encoded TenXTemplates

Input files or glob patterns from which to load JSON-encoded TenXTemplates. (Default: ["path(\"data/sample/templates/*.json\")"])

cacheSize.markdownDescription:
Maximum total byte size of in-memory cached templates

Controls the maximum total byte size of templates held in the in-memory cache. When the cache exceeds this limit, least recently used templates are pruned to a temporary file on disk. Pruned templates are automatically reloaded from disk when accessed again.  Set to 0 to disable pruning and keep all templates in memory. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 20971520)

placeholder.markdownDescription:
Placeholder character for notating TenXObject instance variables

Specifies a character to use when encoding an TenXTemplate to signify the location of a runtime variable value.  For example, for an TenXTemplate containing the following segment:  'ERROR: could not connect to host IP: $.$.$.$'  The '$' entries designate that any TenXObject associated with this template  will substitute the '$' slots with matching values from its individual [vars](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+vars) array (e.g. 0,0,0,127). (Default: $)

maxRecurIndexes.markdownDescription:
Maximum number of template reusable variable value

Controls the maximum number of variable values to reuse when encoding an TenXObject's [vars](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+vars) array to stream. When generating a new  TenXTemplate for an TenXObject instance whose variables contain repetitive values,  the template may indicate that a value is a repetition of a previous variable value and  does not need to be encoded again.  For example, for an TenXObject whose [text](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+text) field contains the following segment: `userID=45dfgdfs4,parentID=45dfgdfs4`, the resulting TenXTemplate can be encoded as: `userID=$,parentID=$1` specifying that the value of the `parentID` variable can be found one slot behind in the TenXObject's [vars array](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+vars), saving the need to encode this value multiple times. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 10)

prefix.markdownDescription:
Template timestamp prefix

Specifies a prefix for an TenXTemplate's set of variable and symbol tokens describing a timestamp (e.g., ${DD/MM/YY HH-mm-ss}). (Default: ()

postfix.markdownDescription:
Template timestamp postfix

Specifies a value appended to an TenXTemplate's and set of variable and symbol tokens describing a timestamp (e.g., ${DD/MM/YY HH-mm-ss}). (Default: ))
